Banking.rate_hike_impact: negative short-term — cost of funds rises, margins compress on variable-rate loans
Banking.rate_cut_impact: positive — stimulates credit demand, improves loan affordability
Banking.dirham_weakness_impact: mixed — pan-African banks (ATW, BOA) lose on currency translation; domestic-only banks neutral
Banking.gdp_growth_impact: positive — economic expansion drives loan demand and reduces defaults
Banking.africa_instability_impact: negative for ATW/BOA — political crises in Côte d'Ivoire, Senegal, or Cameroon directly hit their African subsidiary earnings
Banking.remittance_impact: positive — Moroccan diaspora remittances (~$10bn/yr) support household loan repayment and deposit base

Mining / Fertilizers.commodity_rise_impact: strongly positive — direct revenue uplift for respective metals
Mining / Fertilizers.oil_rise_impact: negative — energy-intensive operations across all mines
Mining / Fertilizers.global_recession_impact: negative — industrial and agricultural commodity demand falls
Mining / Fertilizers.war_conflict_impact: positive for OCP — conflicts in grain-producing regions (Ukraine, Middle East) raise food insecurity, driving fertilizer demand as importing nations rush to secure harvests; also raises phosphate spot prices
Mining / Fertilizers.notes: MNG (Managem) is Morocco's largest silver producer via the Imiter mine — silver price moves directly impact MNG revenue even when gold is flat | MNG's cobalt and copper are sold largely to Chinese EV battery and electronics manufacturers; a Chinese industrial slowdown or EV subsidy cut directly hits MNG volumes | OCP's key products are DAP and MAP fertilizers, not raw phosphate rock — track DAP spot price (CFR Brazil/India) as the most direct revenue signal, not generic 'phosphate price' | OCP benefits when global grain stocks fall or drought hits major producing nations — farmers increase fertilizer application to maximize yield per acre

Telecommunications.rate_hike_impact: low — telecom is defensive with stable cash flows
Telecommunications.gdp_growth_impact: low — defensive stock, not strongly correlated to economic cycle
Telecommunications.notes: IAM's subsidiary Moov Africa operates in 12 sub-Saharan countries — subscriber growth there is a separate earnings driver from Moroccan operations | Most defensive sector on the BVC; moves primarily on company-specific news and dividends

Cement.rate_hike_impact: negative — slows mortgage origination and housing starts
Cement.oil_rise_impact: negative — kiln fuel costs (petcoke and fuel oil) rise directly
Cement.gdp_growth_impact: positive — construction closely tracks economic growth
Cement.government_spend_impact: strongly positive — infrastructure contracts are bulk cement demand
Cement.notes: Morocco co-hosting the 2030 World Cup (with Spain/Portugal) requires ~$3-4bn of stadium, road, rail, and airport construction — multi-year cement demand tailwind through 2029 | A Strait of Hormuz closure raises petcoke and fuel oil import costs for kilns, squeezing cement margins — especially for LHM which is more energy-intensive

Energy / Fuel Distribution.oil_rise_impact: mixed — higher pump prices raise revenue but compress margins if caps prevent full pass-through
Energy / Fuel Distribution.dirham_weakness_impact: negative — imported refined products cost more in MAD
Energy / Fuel Distribution.opec_cut_impact: negative — supply cuts raise crude benchmark, increasing TQM's input costs before any retail price adjustment
Energy / Fuel Distribution.notes: Morocco imports ~90% of its refined fuel needs via sea. A Strait of Hormuz closure or Red Sea disruption causes supply tightness and price spikes. TQM's margin depends on how fast the government adjusts or lifts price caps in response | Middle East wars that threaten Gulf oil supply routes are the single biggest tail risk for TQM — monitor Iran/Gulf tensions and Houthi Red Sea attacks as leading indicators

Real Estate.rate_hike_impact: strongly negative — mortgage affordability falls, buyer demand collapses
Real Estate.rate_cut_impact: strongly positive — unlocks latent housing demand
Real Estate.remittance_impact: positive — diaspora buyers (France, Spain, Italy, Gulf) represent a significant share of ADH/ADI VEFA pre-sales; EUR and Gulf currency strength vs MAD boosts their purchasing power
Real Estate.notes: ADH is heavily exposed to social housing quotas and state subsidies — watch for budget law changes that affect housing subsidy envelopes

Retail / Consumer.inflation_impact: mixed — boosts headline revenue but squeezes margins on non-food items
Retail / Consumer.gdp_growth_impact: positive — higher incomes drive discretionary spending
Retail / Consumer.notes: Wars disrupting global shipping (Red Sea, Black Sea) raise import costs for consumer goods — LBV sources significant inventory internationally

Insurance.rate_hike_impact: positive — higher bond yields boost investment income on the float
Insurance.gdp_growth_impact: positive — higher incomes drive insurance adoption
Insurance.notes: The 2023 Al Hoceima earthquake was a reminder of Morocco's seismic risk — a major event raises claims sharply. Also monitor flood seasons in the Souss-Massa region

Ports / Logistics.dirham_weakness_impact: positive — some fees are USD/EUR denominated
Ports / Logistics.notes: Red Sea / Houthi attack disruptions reroute container ships around the Cape of Good Hope, bypassing Mediterranean ports — this reduces MSA's transit volumes but may boost Atlantic-route calls at Casablanca port | MSA's Jorf Lasfar terminal handles a large share of OCP's phosphate and fertilizer exports — OCP production growth directly drives MSA throughput growth | World Cup 2030 logistics and construction material imports will temporarily spike port volumes through Morocco's terminals

Hospitality.eur_mad_rise_impact: positive — European tourists spend more in MAD terms
Hospitality.global_recession_impact: negative — discretionary travel is first cut
Hospitality.notes: RISMA (Accor-branded hotels: Novotel, Mercure, Sofitel) is the primary listed beneficiary of World Cup 2030. Hotel occupancy and ADR (average daily rate) in Casablanca, Rabat, and Marrakech will surge during the tournament and benefit from infrastructure spending in the years prior | Regional conflicts (Libya, Sahel, Israel-Gaza) affect European travel confidence to North Africa broadly — even if Morocco is stable, perception risk reduces bookings | High oil prices raise European airfares, reducing tourist budget and arrivals — especially from low-cost carrier (Ryanair, easyJet) routes that are price-sensitive

Fintech / Payments.dirham_weakness_impact: positive — significant international revenues in USD/EUR
Fintech / Payments.notes: HPS sells payment software to banks and processors in the Gulf, Africa, and Europe — Gulf bank technology spending is influenced by oil revenues; a sustained low oil price can delay Gulf client contract renewals | Low-liquidity stock; large price moves on thin volume are common
Fintech / Payments.gdp_growth_impact: low direct sensitivity — B2B software contracts are multi-year and largely insulated from short-term economic cycles
Fintech / Payments.gulf_oil_revenue_impact: indirect — Gulf bank IT budgets track oil revenues; sustained low oil prices can delay HPS contract renewals in the Gulf region
Fintech / Payments.africa_fintech_growth_impact: positive — digital payment adoption across Africa expands HPS's addressable market for payment software licensing

Food / Agribusiness.sugar_price_rise_impact: negative — raises input costs if government caps prevent price pass-through
Food / Agribusiness.notes: El Niño typically reduces Brazil sugar cane yields (world's largest producer), pushing global sugar prices higher — negative for CSUMAR as an importer/refiner under state price controls | Poor beet harvests in the Doukkala region (CSUMAR's domestic sugar beet zone) force higher imports at world prices — margin squeeze if retail prices are capped | Black Sea conflicts (Ukraine war) disrupted global sugar supply chains and raised freight costs — any re-escalation would lift CSUMAR's import costs
Food / Agribusiness.drought_impact: negative — poor Moroccan harvests (sugar beet, olive, grain) reduce domestic raw material supply and force higher-cost imports for CSUMAR, DARI, and CSA
Food / Agribusiness.commodity_rise_impact: mixed — commodity-exporting companies (CSA olive oil) gain on higher prices; commodity-importing processors (CSUMAR, DARI) see margin compression
Food / Agribusiness.war_conflict_impact: negative — Black Sea / Middle East conflicts raise durum wheat and sugar freight costs; disrupts global agri-commodity supply chains
Food / Agribusiness.gdp_growth_impact: low — food staples have inelastic demand; premiumisation (organic olive oil, branded pasta) benefits at higher income levels

Healthcare.amo_expansion_impact: strongly positive — mandatory health insurance channels millions of patients to private clinics and creates predictable reimbursement revenue
Healthcare.rate_hike_impact: negative — Akdital's aggressive expansion is debt-financed; higher rates raise capex cost and slow clinic openings
Healthcare.dirham_weakness_impact: negative — medical imaging, surgical equipment, and pharmaceuticals are imported in EUR/USD
Healthcare.gdp_growth_impact: positive — higher incomes shift patients from public to private healthcare
Healthcare.notes: Primary catalyst to monitor: pace of AMO enrollment at CNSS/CNOPS and any changes to reimbursement rate schedules

Power Generation / Utilities.coal_rise_impact: negative — Jorf Lasfar plant's main fuel cost rises; PPA cost-pass-through mechanism partially offsets but with lags
Power Generation / Utilities.dirham_weakness_impact: negative — coal imports are USD-denominated, raising MAD-equivalent fuel costs
Power Generation / Utilities.onee_stress_impact: high risk — ONEE is the sole power buyer under the PPA; ONEE financial difficulties or delayed payments directly threaten revenue
Power Generation / Utilities.energy_transition_impact: long-term negative — regulatory coal phaseout targets pressure this asset; green energy mandates could alter PPA terms
Power Generation / Utilities.notes: Very defensive stock — long-term PPA with ONEE gives revenue visibility; main risks are coal price spikes and counterparty credit of ONEE

Automobile Distribution.rate_hike_impact: negative — auto loans become expensive, discretionary vehicle purchases deferred
Automobile Distribution.gdp_growth_impact: strongly positive — vehicle sales are highly income-elastic
Automobile Distribution.oil_rise_impact: mixed — higher fuel costs may shift demand to smaller cars, but fleet sales (trucks, Iveco commercial vehicles) are less price-sensitive
Automobile Distribution.dirham_weakness_impact: negative — vehicle prices in MAD rise when EUR/USD strengthens

Consumer Goods / FMCG.inflation_impact: mixed — raises input costs but staple products retain pricing power
Consumer Goods / FMCG.oil_rise_impact: negative — petrochemical-derived packaging and transport costs rise
Consumer Goods / FMCG.gdp_growth_impact: positive — higher incomes increase FMCG volumes and premiumization
Consumer Goods / FMCG.africa_growth_impact: positive — West African middle-class growth drives export revenues for cleaning and food products

Real Estate / REIT.rate_hike_impact: negative — higher discount rates compress REIT NAV; also raises refinancing costs
Real Estate / REIT.rate_cut_impact: positive — REIT valuations re-rate upward as cap rates compress
Real Estate / REIT.gdp_growth_impact: positive — rising consumer spending improves tenant sales, reducing vacancy risk
Real Estate / REIT.e_commerce_impact: negative long-term — online retail growth reduces foot traffic at physical shopping centers
Real Estate / REIT.notes: Yield stock — dividend yield relative to government bond yield is the main valuation metric. Watch 10-year Morocco bond yield as direct competitor for income-seeking investors

Pharmaceuticals.amo_expansion_impact: positive — more insured patients means higher reimbursed prescription volumes
Pharmaceuticals.dirham_weakness_impact: negative — APIs sourced from China and India are priced in USD/EUR
Pharmaceuticals.api_disruption_impact: high risk — supply chain shocks (as seen during COVID-19) raise input costs sharply or halt production
Pharmaceuticals.drug_price_regulation_impact: constraining — government caps on medicine prices limit pricing power even when input costs rise

Construction Materials.gdp_growth_impact: positive — more construction means more paint demand
Construction Materials.oil_rise_impact: negative — most paint raw materials are petrochemical derivatives (resins, solvents, pigment dispersants)
Construction Materials.government_spend_impact: positive — public infrastructure, school, hospital renovation programs are large Colorado customers
Construction Materials.rate_hike_impact: negative — slows housing starts and private renovation projects

Beverages.tourism_impact: positive — hotel, restaurant, and bar trade drives beer volumes in summer and during events
Beverages.regulation_impact: high risk — any tightening of alcohol sale rules, licensing, or taxation is a direct revenue hit
Beverages.barley_rise_impact: negative — main brewing input cost rises
Beverages.gdp_growth_impact: mild positive — beer is affordable and not strongly income-elastic
Beverages.notes: Monopoly position and Heineken affiliate make BRM a defensive yield stock; dividend policy is the primary valuation driver

Electrical Equipment / Cables.copper_rise_impact: negative short-term — main raw material; margins compress before contracts reprice
Electrical Equipment / Cables.renewables_capex_impact: strongly positive — solar farms, wind projects, and grid upgrades require large cable volumes
Electrical Equipment / Cables.automotive_hub_impact: positive — Renault and Stellantis Morocco plants drive local wire harness demand

IT Distribution.gdp_growth_impact: positive — corporate investment drives IT upgrades
IT Distribution.dirham_weakness_impact: negative — IT products priced in USD/EUR; weaker MAD compresses distributor margins
IT Distribution.digitalization_impact: positive — government and private sector digital transformation programs expand addressable market

Aluminium / Industrial Materials.electricity_cost_impact: high sensitivity — aluminum production is one of the most electricity-intensive industrial processes
Aluminium / Industrial Materials.china_dumping_impact: negative — Chinese overcapacity and subsidized exports depress global aluminum prices and undercut local producers
Aluminium / Industrial Materials.morocco_industrial_hub_impact: positive — growth of automotive and aerospace manufacturing in Morocco (Renault, Stellantis, aerospace suppliers) drives local aluminum demand

Steel.scrap_price_rise_impact: negative — main raw material for Sonasid's electric arc furnace
Steel.electricity_cost_impact: high — electric arc furnace is extremely electricity-intensive; ONE (Office National de l'Electricité) tariff decisions directly set Sonasid's cost structure
Steel.notes: Chinese steel overcapacity leads to subsidized exports at below-market prices — when China floods global markets with cheap steel, it undercuts Sonasid's pricing in the Moroccan and regional market | Conflicts that destroy infrastructure create post-war reconstruction demand for rebar — historically positive for regional steel producers once the acute phase ends; ongoing wars raise scrap supply from destroyed equipment | EU carbon border adjustment could eventually raise costs for Moroccan steel exports to Europe if Morocco's carbon pricing does not align with EU standards
